final_answer: Secure the WebSocket channel with TLS (wss://) to protect tokens and message integrity (N8:3:9a4f1bd4). Issue short‑lived JWT or opaque tokens bound to the client session and store recent token IDs (jti) in a cache to enforce one‑time use, rejecting reused tokens (N8:4:6aced31c, N5:4:b8a1d3d5). Attach a monotonically increasing sequence number to each event (N1:0:5f087a3d) and have clients track the last sequence they processed, discarding any event with a lower or equal number (N1:1:db770d6b). On reconnect, the client sends its last processed sequence number; the server resends any missed events starting from the next number (N8:2:2a55e671). Maintain a short‑term server‑side deduplication cache keyed by event ID to drop duplicate messages that may arrive due to reconnection or network reordering (N8:6:1b0068fe). Use WebSocket ping/pong keep‑alives to detect dead connections promptly and trigger reconnection (N8:7:c7fa53d0). Implement application‑level ACK/NACK so the client can acknowledge received events and request retransmission of gaps (N5:6:9083ce5a). Finally, design event handlers to be idempotent so that any accidental duplicate processing has no side effects (N1:3:18b857db).
